## Findings



### CORVUS-001 — Excessive description length in 'create_campaign'

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **MEDIUM** |
| OWASP | MCP03 |
| Tool | `create_campaign` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 80% |

Description is 1144 chars (limit 1000). May contain hidden content.




**Remediation:** Keep descriptions concise; review for hidden instructions.

---

### CORVUS-002 — Excessive description length in 'create_adset'

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **MEDIUM** |
| OWASP | MCP03 |
| Tool | `create_adset` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 80% |

Description is 1098 chars (limit 1000). May contain hidden content.




**Remediation:** Keep descriptions concise; review for hidden instructions.

### CORVUS-003 — High entropy description in 'search_ads_archive'

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **LOW** |
| OWASP | MCP03 |
| Tool | `search_ads_archive` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 20% |

Unusual character distribution may indicate obfuscated content.




**Remediation:** Review description for base64-encoded or otherwise obfuscated payloads.

### CORVUS-004 — Parameter 'ab_test_control_setups' in 'create_campaign' has no type constraint

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **LOW** |
| OWASP | EXT02 |
| Tool | `create_campaign` |
| Parameter | `ab_test_control_setups` |
| Confidence | 80% |

Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.




**Remediation:** Add an explicit type to parameter 'ab_test_control_setups'.

### CORVUS-016 — Auth Bypass — 'debug_image_download' uses a restricted-access naming convention

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **HIGH** |
| OWASP | MCP07 |
| Tool | `debug_image_download` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 75% |

Tool name 'debug_image_download' follows a naming pattern (admin_, internal_, debug_, etc.) that implies restricted access, but no authentication enforcement is visible.




**Remediation:** Restrict access-controlled tools using server-side auth checks. Do not rely on naming conventions as a security boundary.

---

### CORVUS-017 — Token Exposure — credential in response in 'get_ad_accounts'

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **CRITICAL** |
| OWASP | MCP01 |
| Tool | `get_ad_accounts` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 85% |

Tool response contains credential in response.



**Evidence:**
```
{
  "data": "{\n  \"error\": {\n    \"message\": \"HTTP Error: 400\",\n    \"details\": {\n      \"error\": {\n        \"message\": \"Malformed access token\",\n        \"type\": \"OAuthException\",\n        \"code\": 190,\n        \"fbtrace_id\": \"AhVzysKVgdqOLfr9FpFrnf_\"\n      }\n    },\n    \"full_response\": {\n      \"headers\": {\n        \"debug-link\": \"https://www.meta.com/debug/?mid=
```


**Remediation:** Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

| Tool | `get_ad_accounts` |
| Parameter | `—` |

**Remediation:** Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

### CORVUS-047 — SSRF (timing) — 'get_login_link.access_token' delayed on SSRF payload

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **HIGH** |
| OWASP | EXT04 |
| Tool | `get_login_link` |
| Parameter | `access_token` |
| Confidence | 70% |

Call with payload 'http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/' took 3.3s vs baseline 1.0s — server likely attempted the outbound request before timing out.


**Payload:**
```
http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/
```



**Remediation:** Block outbound requests to RFC-1918 and link-local ranges. Use an allowlist for permitted URL schemes and hosts.

---

### CORVUS-048 — initialize accepts protocol version downgrade

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **MEDIUM** |
| OWASP | MCP07 |
| Tool | `—` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 75% |

The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.



**Evidence:**
```
Accepted versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']
```


**Remediation:** Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

---

### CORVUS-049 — initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **LOW** |
| OWASP | MCP07 |
| Tool | `—` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 65% |

The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.



**Evidence:**
```
protocolVersion: 42 (integer) → server returned serverInfo
```


**Remediation:** Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

---

### CORVUS-050 — MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **INFO** |
| OWASP | EXT10 |
| Tool | `—` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 90% |

26 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.




**Remediation:** Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

---

### CORVUS-051 — Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **MEDIUM** |
| OWASP | EXT13 |
| Tool | `—` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 65% |

tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.


**Payload:**
```
../../../../etc/passwd
```



**Remediation:** Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

---

### CORVUS-052 — Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **MEDIUM** |
| OWASP | EXT01 |
| Tool | `—` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 60% |

The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.




**Remediation:** Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

---

### CORVUS-053 — Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Severity | **LOW** |
| OWASP | EXT01 |
| Tool | `—` |
| Parameter | `—` |
| Confidence | 65% |

Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.


**Payload:**
```
{"method":"tools/call","params":"not-an-object"}
```



**Remediation:** Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

---



*Generated by [Corvus](https://github.com/CobaltoSec/corvus)*
